input.issue_title: When using MSVC to compile v12.0.0, many compilation warnings are generated
input.issue_body:
`size_t` and `unsigned __int64` do not match when compiling with x86, resulting in the compiler generating a lot of compilation information
```
fmt\format.h(2509,32): warning C4244: 'argument': conversion from 'unsigned __int64' to 'size_t', possible loss of data [app.vcxproj]
fmt\format.h(3505): message : see reference to function template instantiation'OutputIt fmt::v12::detail::write_fixed<Char,fmt::v12::detail::fallback_digit_grouping<Char>,OutputIt,fmt::v12::detail::dragonbox::decimal_fp<float>>(OutputIt,const DecimalFP &,int,Char,const fmt::v12::format_specs &,fmt::v12::sign,fmt::v12::locale_ref)' being compiled [app.vcxproj]
         with
         [
             OutputIt=fmt::v12::basic_appender<char>,
             Char=char,
             DecimalFP=fmt::v12::detail::dragonbox::decimal_fp<float>
         ]
fmt\format.h(3649): message : see reference to function template instantiation 'OutputIt fmt::v12::detail::write<Char,fmt::v12::basic_appender<char>,T,0>(OutputIt,T)' being compiled [app.vcxproj]
         with
         [
             OutputIt=fmt::v12::basic_appender<char>,
             Char=char,
             T=float
         ]
fmt\base.h(2541): message : see reference to function template instantiation 'void fmt::v12::detail::default_arg_formatter<char>::operator ()<float,0>(T)' being compiled [app.vcxproj]
         with
         [
             T=float
         ]
fmt\base.h(2541): message : see reference to function template instantiation 'void fmt::v12::detail::default_arg_formatter<char>::operator ()<float,0>(T)' being compiled [app.vcxproj]
         with
         [
             T=float
         ]
fmt\format-inl.h(1460): message : see reference to function template instantiation 'void fmt::v12::basic_format_arg<Context>::visit<fmt::v12::detail::default_arg_formatter<char>>(Visitor &&)const' being compiled [app.vcxproj]
         with
         [
             Context=fmt::v12::context,
             Visitor=fmt::v12::detail::default_arg_formatter<char>

input.issue_title: Compile error: partial specialization of struct fmt::v11::formatter

input.pr_body:
Just a 1-line change to reuse the `fmt::detail::copy()` method instead of manually loop over each character. For that, some methods need to be moved around.

Demonstration of the speedup in https://github.com/fmtlib/format-benchmark/issues/35 . Arguably `vector<char>` is a rare type, but it might be useful for other things (custom string types...?)

To make tests pass, I need to  check whether the iterator type is move-assignable (`throwing_iterator` is not assignable), although I'd argue that in practice iterators ought to be move-assignable and that test is actually invalid.

input.pr_title: Move a few classes into an anonymous namespace

input.pr_body:
Fixes #4569.

This reverts the change from malloc/free to std::malloc/std::free introduced in [#4477](https://github.com/fmtlib/fmt/pull/4477).
Using the std:: versions  broke compatibility with global allocator overrides (e.g. mimalloc), since std::malloc can’t be intercepted.

Fix

Switched back to global malloc / free.

ground_truth.known_issues: PR was reverted — agent should identify the problematic change.
